content: Human language learning's reflexion (reflection) practice, where learners correct their own writing errors by reviewing output against implicit linguistic norms, can be adapted as a lightweight framework for improving LLM self-reflection mechanisms that outperforms complex multi-perspective contrastive methods. This framework treats LLM reasoning refinement the same way high school writing workshops teach reflective error correction: iterative self-review of generated output against anchored standards rather than generating multiple inconsistent perspectives to find flaws.
